metrics.narrative: Amy Snider quantified the pain in specific, dated terms on the 14 Jan 2026 discovery call: roughly 65% of contracts are vendor paper vs. 35% Zycus paper, and the team manually tracks ~15 renewals a month on an Excel spreadsheet because GEP Worldwide has no renewal-status dashboard. She also flagged that PO creation post-contract-execution is fully manual in GEP today. These are concrete, buyer-voiced operational metrics, not rep inference.

champion.narrative: Amy Snider is cooperative and explicitly open to change ('I'm not married to anything... I'm more than open to fixing anything that we can', 14 Jan 2026), and she brought Bernardo Silva into the 16 Jan 2026 demo. This is evaluator engagement, not confirmed internal advocacy: there is no evidence she has championed Zycus to the new CEO, to Finance, or to IT, and no contact has occurred in the ~175 days since the demo to test that advocacy.

competition.narrative: GEP Worldwide is the incumbent and primary competitor: Amy Snider stated the underlying process 'is working well' but the platform itself is unstable, framing this as a reliability-driven replacement rather than a wholesale process failure. Four other named competitors sit on the Salesforce Competitors field (Coupa, iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba) with no call evidence naming any of them directly; Amy did recall Zycus was evaluated ~4 years ago and 'did not make it to the top 3' at that time, without naming who won.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is specific and buyer-owned: Amy Snider described constant bugs/glitches in GEP Worldwide requiring weekly support tickets ('we're constantly putting in support tickets for something silly that has changed from one day to the next'), no visibility into renewal status outside Excel, manual cross-name access for her two-person purchasing team, and fully manual PO creation after contract execution. This is the clearest, most durable signal on the deal.

Single-threaded — only Amy Snider (Global Director of Procurement) and Bernardo Silva (AP Specialist) have ever engaged; no contact above procurement level has occurred on this opportunity.

Live five-way field on record — GEP Worldwide (incumbent), Coupa, iValua, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba are all logged as competitors, and GEP's underlying process was described as 'working well' by the buyer despite its reliability problems.

deal_momentum.text: No verified buyer engagement in ~175 days — the last confirmed two-way buyer touch was the 16 Jan 2026 demo; the only activity since is a rep-side Next Step update logged 6 Jul 2026, awaiting a buyer reply.
Close date unsupported — 29 Jul 2026 is 20 days out with no paper process, no commercials discussion, and an explicitly delayed decision.

customer_commitment.text: Buyer openness is evaluator-neutral, not Zycus-specific — Amy Snider said 'I'm not married to anything... I'm more than open to fixing anything' (14 Jan 2026), which reflects a fair evaluation stance rather than confirmed advocacy for Zycus.
Light multi-threading exists — Bernardo Silva (AP Specialist) joined the 16 Jan 2026 demo alongside Amy Snider.
